llm_ranked.evidence_en: unified fast-to-slow architecture for vision-language-action models
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: unified fast-to-slow architecture for vision-language-action models
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Fast-Slow dual system VLA models suffer from frequency dilemma and information loss. UniFS proposes a unified hierarchical architecture that stratifies feature transmission across multiple timescales. It solves semantic drift and improves manipulation precision by preserving intermediate features. Experiments demonstrate efficiency gains and performance improvements over existing VLA architectures.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Existing Fast-Slow VLA models face a frequency dilemma and discard rich intermediate features.
llm_ranked.method_en: UniFS introduces a unified hierarchical architecture with multi-timescale feature stratification.
llm_ranked.result_en: The architecture improves efficiency and manipulation precision over state-of-the-art VLA models.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: UniFS provides a principled solution to the frequency-information tradeoff in VLA architectures.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: vision language action model for robot control

llm_ranked.evidence_en: safety benchmark for VLA models
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: safety benchmark for VLA models
llm_ranked.tldr_en: LIBERO-Safety introduces a parametric safety benchmark for Vision-Language-Action models, procedurally generating safety-critical scenarios. It develops a keypose-driven data generation pipeline to create a large-scale dataset of collision-free demonstrations. A systematic cross-paradigm evaluation of eight VLA and two embodied foundation models reveals critical safety gaps. This work provides a standardized evaluation protocol for safety in VLA manipulation.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Safety of VLA models under strict constraints is largely unverified.
llm_ranked.method_en: Procedurally generates safety-critical scenarios and uses a keypose-driven pipeline for large-scale demonstration collection.
llm_ranked.result_en: Curates 19,664 collision-free demonstrations and evaluates eight VLA and two embodied foundation models.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Reveals critical generalizability issues in VLA safety and provides a structured evaluation benchmark.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: Evaluation of foundation models for robot control policies

llm_ranked.evidence_en: deterministic policy gradient for flow matching policies
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: deterministic policy gradient for flow matching policies
llm_ranked.tldr_en: FlowDPG addresses the challenge of applying policy gradient to flow matching policies for robotic manipulation. It proposes a DDPG-style method that distills critic gradients into the velocity field, bypassing backpropagation through time. The method combines demonstration-driven velocity with critic-driven correction. It enables efficient reinforcement learning for flow matching policies.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Applying policy gradient to flow matching policies is computationally prohibitive due to backpropagation through time.
llm_ranked.method_en: FlowDPG distills critic gradient into velocity field at training time, avoiding BPTT.
llm_ranked.result_en: Enables efficient RL training for flow matching policies in real-world manipulation.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Provides a practical RL method for flow matching models.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: reinforcement learning for robot control

llm_ranked.evidence_en: automated test oracle generation for VLA models
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: automated test oracle generation for VLA models
llm_ranked.tldr_en: MANGO automates the generation of test oracles for Vision-Language-Action models. It uses a multi-agent framework to generate oracles that assess both final outcomes and intermediate behaviors. The approach reduces human effort and improves scalability. It addresses limitations of manually constructed symbolic oracles.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Existing VLA testing relies on costly, task-specific symbolic oracles.
llm_ranked.method_en: Multi-agent framework that automatically generates scalable test oracles.
llm_ranked.result_en: Provides insight into intermediate behavior and fault localization.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Reduces human effort in VLA evaluation.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: Evaluation of foundation models for robot control policies

llm_ranked.evidence_en: proposes UniviewVLA a multiview VLA model with world modeling for occlusion handling in robot manipulation
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: proposes UniviewVLA a multiview VLA model with world modeling for occlusion handling in robot manipulation
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Occluded tasks remain a bottleneck in robot manipulation. Existing solutions require additional cameras or explicit 3D reconstruction with high cost. UniviewVLA proposes a unified multiview Vision-Language-Action model with world modeling that infers multiview scene evolution for action prediction from only standard two-camera observations. By generating multiview future views, it reveals occluded cues and models future scene evolution, leading to improved manipulation performance without extra hardware.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: To overcome occlusion bottlenecks in robot manipulation without extra hardware or high computational cost.
llm_ranked.method_en: UniviewVLA is a unified multiview Vision-Language-Action model with world modeling that generates multiview future views from standard camera inputs.
llm_ranked.result_en: Shows improved performance on occluded manipulation tasks by leveraging generated multiview future views.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: UniviewVLA effectively handles occlusions using only standard camera inputs, advancing VLA models.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: How can vision language action models be used for generalist robot manipulation?

llm_ranked.evidence_en: ZeroDex enables zero-shot long-horizon dexterous manipulation using VLM reasoning
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: ZeroDex enables zero-shot long-horizon dexterous manipulation using VLM reasoning
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Long-horizon dexterous manipulation remains challenging. ZeroDex uses a vision-language model to ground language instructions into 3D task plans from multi-view RGB images, without training an end-to-end policy. It lifts 2D keypoints to 3D via multi-view fusion and supports both pick-and-place and tool-use. Experiments show zero-shot generalization to novel objects and tasks.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: To achieve zero-shot long-horizon dexterous manipulation without training end-to-end policies.
llm_ranked.method_en: ZeroDex uses VLM to produce reference-frame task grounding and 2D keypoints, then lifts them to 3D via multi-view fusion and ray voting.
llm_ranked.result_en: Demonstrates zero-shot generalization in pick-and-place and tool-use tasks.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: ZeroDex provides an efficient framework for dexterous manipulation without policy training.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: robot manipulation with dexterous hands

llm_ranked.evidence_en: VLA model that uses action consequence context for better generalization
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: VLA model that uses action consequence context for better generalization
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Reflective VLA improves generalization of vision-language-action models by conditioning decisions on a context of observation-action-consequence triplets, rather than being purely reactive. This allows the model to disambiguate embodiment-specific factors like calibration and bias. Experiments show improved out-of-distribution performance on robot manipulation tasks, demonstrating a key mechanism for generalist robot policies.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Reactive VLA models overfit to training environments and generalize poorly due to unobserved embodiment-specific factors.
llm_ranked.method_en: Reflective VLA uses a context of past observation-action-consequence triplets to condition action prediction.
llm_ranked.result_en: Better generalization across different robots and environment configurations.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: In-context action consequences enable VLA models to adapt to embodiment variations.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: vision language action model for robot control

llm_ranked.evidence_en: VLA model generalization via in-context world modeling
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: VLA model generalization via in-context world modeling
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models often fail to generalize to novel camera viewpoints or robot morphologies because they ignore system configuration. In-Context World Modeling (ICWM) treats system identification as an in-context adaptation problem, enabling policies to infer essential system variables from a short history of self-generated task-agnostic interactions. Experiments show significantly improved generalization across diverse setups without fine-tuning. This framework reduces the data cost for deploying VLA models in new environments.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: VLA models fail to generalize to novel setups like altered camera viewpoints or robot morphologies due to assuming fixed execution context.
llm_ranked.method_en: ICWM enables robot policies to autonomously infer system variables from short self-generated task-agnostic interactions via in-context adaptation.
llm_ranked.result_en: Experiments demonstrate significantly improved generalization across diverse setups without need for data-intensive fine-tuning.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: In-context adaptation reduces dependence on extensive fine-tuning, making VLA models more practical for real-world deployment.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: vision language action model for robot control

llm_ranked.evidence_en: Self-consistent video generation for evaluating robot foundation models
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: Self-consistent video generation for evaluating robot foundation models
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Real-world evaluation of generalist robot manipulation policies is expensive and slow. SC3-Eval adapts a pre-trained video foundation model into a policy evaluator by enforcing forward-inverse dynamics consistency, multi-view consistency, and distribution consistency. Experiments show it can accurately rank policies' real-world performance using only simulated rollouts. This provides a scalable evaluation method for robotic foundation models.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Evaluating generalist robot manipulation policies in the real world is expensive, slow, and difficult to scale.
llm_ranked.method_en: SC3-Eval enforces forward-inverse dynamics, multi-view, and distribution consistency to adapt a video foundation model as a policy evaluator.
llm_ranked.result_en: Accurate ranking of robot policies on real-world tasks using only simulated video rollouts.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Self-consistent video generation offers a scalable alternative to real-world evaluation for foundation model policies.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: Evaluation of foundation models for robot control policies

llm_ranked.evidence_en: dexterous full-hand tactile benchmark with egocentric vision
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: dexterous full-hand tactile benchmark with egocentric vision
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Dexterous manipulation with tactile sensing lacks unified benchmarks. HT-Bench provides a large-scale multi-task benchmark with 10M RGB frames and 7.8M tactile frames across 226 tasks. It evaluates tactile representations on contact geometry encoding, cross-modal alignment, and task performance. This enables systematic study of learning dexterous manipulation with full-hand tactile feedback.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: A universal benchmark for dexterous tactile representation learning is missing due to sensor diversity.
llm_ranked.method_en: HT-Bench collects egocentric vision paired with full-hand tactile data across 226 tasks and evaluates representations on multiple criteria.
llm_ranked.result_en: HT-Bench offers a large-scale dataset and evaluation protocol for dexterous full-hand tactile sensing.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Egocentric vision with tactile data is a scalable direction for advancing dexterous manipulation benchmarks.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: robot manipulation with dexterous hands

llm_ranked.evidence_en: flatness-preserving optimization to prevent instruction blindness in VLA models
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: flatness-preserving optimization to prevent instruction blindness in VLA models
llm_ranked.tldr_en: VLA models finetuned on limited robot data often ignore language instructions due to sharp loss landscapes. The paper proposes flatness-preserving optimization that learns a flatter minimum, making the policy robust to perturbations. Experiments show that this method retains instruction-following ability without additional data. This work directly addresses a key failure mode of VLA models.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Finetuning VLA models on small robot datasets degrades pretrained representations, causing instruction blindness.
llm_ranked.method_en: Flatness-preserving optimization encourages the model to find flat minima during finetuning, improving robustness.
llm_ranked.result_en: The proposed method maintains instruction-following performance even with limited robot data.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Flatness-aware finetuning is a simple fix to preserve language grounding in VLA policies.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: vision language action model for robot control

llm_ranked.evidence_en: failure-driven data augmentation for VLA policies to handle novel objects
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: failure-driven data augmentation for VLA policies to handle novel objects
llm_ranked.tldr_en: VLA policies fail on novel objects due to distribution shift. Pose6DAug turns successful episodes into targeted demonstrations by swapping the manipulated object while preserving trajectories. This augmentation creates diverse training examples without new data collection. Experiments show improved generalization to out-of-distribution objects, making VLA policies more robust.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Collecting teleoperation data for every novel object is expensive; VLA policies need better generalization.
llm_ranked.method_en: Pose6DAug swaps manipulated objects in successful episodes using multi-view observations and consistent trajectories.
llm_ranked.result_en: The augmentation significantly improves VLA policy success on novel objects without additional data.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Failure-driven augmentation leverages existing data to enhance VLA generalization efficiently.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: vision language action model for robot control

llm_ranked.evidence_en: evaluation protocol to measure commonsense knowledge retention in VLA models
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: evaluation protocol to measure commonsense knowledge retention in VLA models
llm_ranked.tldr_en: It is unclear how much knowledge VLA models retain after finetuning on robotics data. Act2Answer adapts VLM knowledge benchmarks to VLA evaluation by requiring answers through actions. This protocol yields an action-grounded success rate that separates knowledge from control confounds. The work provides a method to evaluate foundation models for robot control policies.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Current evaluation conflates missing knowledge with poor control in VLA models.
llm_ranked.method_en: Act2Answer transforms each question into a tabletop episode where the agent selects an answer via object placement.
llm_ranked.result_en: The protocol measures knowledge retention in VLA models, revealing significant degradation after finetuning.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Act2Answer enables principled evaluation of knowledge in VLA policies for robot control.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: Evaluation of foundation models for robot control policies

llm_ranked.evidence_en: vector-quantized action tokenization for multi-task VLA manipulation
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: vector-quantized action tokenization for multi-task VLA manipulation
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Multi-task robot manipulation from demonstration requires handling multiple action modes. VQActFlow tokenizes continuous actions into a discrete codebook and generates code sequences via Variational Flow Matching. This representation separates action modes and maintains explicit preferences throughout generation. Experiments show improved multi-task performance and task selection accuracy.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Single networks struggle to select among diverse action modes in multi-task manipulation.
llm_ranked.method_en: VQActFlow tokenizes action chunks into a discrete codebook and uses flow matching to generate code sequences.
llm_ranked.result_en: VQActFlow outperforms baselines on multi-task manipulation benchmarks with language and visual conditioning.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Discrete action tokenization helps VLA models handle multimodal demonstration distributions effectively.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: vision language action model for robot control

llm_ranked.evidence_en: reinforcement learning for discrete diffusion VLA models
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: reinforcement learning for discrete diffusion VLA models
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Discrete Diffusion VLA models unify vision, language, and action but have only been trained with supervised fine-tuning. dVLA-RL extends them with reinforcement learning over denoising trajectories. This combines iterative refinement with RL objectives, improving policy performance on complex tasks. The work opens the door to RL training for discrete VLA architectures.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Discrete Diffusion VLAs lack reinforcement learning training, limiting their potential.
llm_ranked.method_en: dVLA-RL applies RL over denoising trajectories of a discrete diffusion VLA model.
llm_ranked.result_en: RL training improves task success rates compared to SFT-only baselines on manipulation benchmarks.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Reinforcement learning can enhance discrete diffusion VLA models for generalist robot control.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: vision language action model for robot control

llm_ranked.evidence_en: boosting policy efficiency of VLA models by reducing inference calls
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: boosting policy efficiency of VLA models by reducing inference calls
llm_ranked.tldr_en: VLA models face efficiency bottlenecks in real-world deployment due to many forward passes. PolicyTrim addresses intrinsic policy efficiency by increasing action chunk length and reducing redundant actions. It analyzes planning reliability and action redundancy to produce leaner action sequences. Experiments show significant reduction in inference calls while maintaining task success.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Current VLA models are inefficient in physical steps and inference calls, hindering real-time deployment.
llm_ranked.method_en: PolicyTrim identifies and trims redundant action steps while extending reliable action chunks.
llm_ranked.result_en: PolicyTrim reduces total inference calls by up to 50% without sacrificing manipulation success.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Improving policy efficiency is key for deploying VLA models in real-world robotic systems.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: vision language action model for robot control

llm_ranked.evidence_en: World value models as foundation for generalist robotic manipulation
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: World value models as foundation for generalist robotic manipulation
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Generalist value models are important for scaling robot policy learning from large data. Existing value models use VLM backbones lacking temporal understanding. This paper marries world models with value estimation to construct World Value Models, which naturally handle temporal modeling and future planning. The approach learns generalizable value functions from mixed-quality data. Experiments show improved value estimation and policy learning, advancing robot foundation models.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Current VLM-based value models lack temporal modeling for accurate value estimation.
llm_ranked.method_en: Combine world models with value estimation to create temporally-aware generalist value functions.
llm_ranked.result_en: World Value Models outperform VLM-based counterparts in value estimation and downstream policy learning.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: World models provide a strong foundation for learning generalizable value functions in manipulation.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: robot foundation model for generalist robotics

llm_ranked.evidence_en: Imitation learning from demonstrations using discrete skill abstractions
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: Imitation learning from demonstrations using discrete skill abstractions
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Skill-based imitation learning for long-horizon manipulation suffers from weak visual grounding and precision errors. This paper proposes Aligned Refinement Policy (ARP), a discrete-skill framework that enhances visual-semantic alignment through cross-modal attention and iterative refinement of skill decoding. Experiments on various manipulation tasks show improved skill selection and action precision. ARP bridges the gap between discrete skills and continuous control.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Existing discrete skill methods lack visual grounding and suffer from precision loss in action generation.
llm_ranked.method_en: ARP couples semantic grounding with iterative refinement for discrete skill-based imitation learning.
llm_ranked.result_en: ARP outperforms prior methods in skill selection and execution accuracy on manipulation benchmarks.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Visual alignment and iterative refinement enhance the effectiveness of skill-based imitation learning.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: imitation learning from human demonstrations for robots

llm_ranked.evidence_en: vision-language-action model decoupling for imitation learning
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: vision-language-action model decoupling for imitation learning
llm_ranked.tldr_en: End-to-end VLA models couple semantic reasoning and spatial control, causing an alignment bottleneck in imitation learning. SVP-IL decouples these by using vision-language foundation models to extract zero-shot geometric masks as Spatial Visual Prompts, injected into a continuous action generator via feature-level fusion. This improves target disambiguation and data efficiency. The method demonstrates better precision in language-conditioned manipulation tasks.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: End-to-end VLA models couple semantic and spatial reasoning, limiting precise target disambiguation in data-constrained imitation learning.
llm_ranked.method_en: SVP-IL leverages vision-language foundation models to create zero-shot geometric masks as Spatial Visual Prompts, decoupling semantics from action generation.
llm_ranked.result_en: The decoupled architecture improves target disambiguation and data efficiency in language-conditioned imitation learning tasks.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: SVP-IL shows that decoupling semantics from spatial control enhances VLA model performance in data-constrained settings.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: vision language action model for robot control

llm_ranked.evidence_en: benchmarking action spaces in RL for vision-based manipulation
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: benchmarking action spaces in RL for vision-based manipulation
llm_ranked.tldr_en: This study benchmarks four action spaces (pose increment, pose velocity, joint position increment, joint velocity) for vision-based RL manipulation in picking and pushing tasks. Policies are trained in simulation and deployed via sim-to-real transfer. Results show joint velocity action space yields the best smoothness and task performance. Practical guidance for RL practitioners is provided. The work highlights the impact of action representation on sim-to-real success.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: The choice of action space significantly affects motion smoothness, safety, and performance in real-world RL, yet it is underexplored.
llm_ranked.method_en: Benchmarking four action spaces (pose increment/velocity, joint position increment/velocity) on vision-based picking and pushing tasks with sim-to-real transfer.
llm_ranked.result_en: Joint velocity action space achieves best smoothness and task performance across both tasks in sim-to-real deployment.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Action space representation critically influences sim-to-real performance; joint velocity is recommended for vision-based manipulation RL.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: reinforcement learning for robot control

llm_ranked.evidence_en: robot velocity fields as probability flows for foundation models
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: robot velocity fields as probability flows for foundation models
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Flow as Flow models robot velocity fields as probability flows via flow matching, enabling embodiment-agnostic motion representations for cross-embodiment data. Unlike prior work using sparse keypoint displacements, this formulation captures dense velocity fields consistent with continuous-time motion. The framework generates efficient, high-quality robot flows for object manipulation. This advances robotic foundation models by leveraging heterogeneous data through a unified representation.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Previous robot flow representations as sparse keypoint displacements do not match the continuous-time nature of motions, limiting cross-embodiment learning.
llm_ranked.method_en: Flow as Flow models robot velocity fields as probability flows using a flow matching formulation, producing dense embodiment-agnostic motion representations.
llm_ranked.result_en: The framework achieves efficient and high-quality robot flow generation, outperforming sparse keypoint methods in object manipulation tasks.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Flow as Flow provides a unified continuous-time representation for cross-embodiment data, enabling better robotic foundation model training.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: robot foundation model for generalist robotics

llm_ranked.evidence_en: evaluation framework for visuomotor policy generalization
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: evaluation framework for visuomotor policy generalization
llm_ranked.tldr_en: This paper argues that current evaluation of visuomotor policies relies on interpolation over known distribution shifts, not true generalization. It proposes inductive generalization: testing policies on progressively harder out-of-distribution task variants, analogous to axis-based evaluation in language models. A reusable and formal evaluation framework is provided. This work enables more rigorous assessment of robot foundation models and control policies.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Current visuomotor policy evaluation measures interpolation on known distributions, failing to capture true generalization capabilities.
llm_ranked.method_en: Propose inductive generalization evaluation: test policies on progressively harder out-of-distribution task variants, with a reusable formal framework.
llm_ranked.result_en: The framework reveals inherent generalization limitations in visuomotor policies, analogous to those in language models.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Inductive generalization evaluation provides a more rigorous measure of policy generalization, crucial for developing capable robot foundation models.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: Evaluation of foundation models for robot control policies

llm_ranked.evidence_en: human hand demonstrations for imitation learning with latent reasoning
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: human hand demonstrations for imitation learning with latent reasoning
llm_ranked.tldr_en: LaST-HD extends reasoning-before-acting VLA by aligning human-hand and robot demonstrations in a shared latent reasoning space using an action-conditioned world model trained on unpaired data. This goes beyond geometric retargeting to align physical dynamics between human and robot manipulation. The method enables robust transfer from scalable human demonstrations to robot policies, improving generalization across embodiments. This advances imitation learning from human demonstrations for robots.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Human-hand demonstrations provide scalable data for robot learning, but geometric retargeting alone fails to align physical dynamics across embodiments.
llm_ranked.method_en: LaST-HD trains an action-conditioned world model on unpaired human-hand and robot trajectories to align them in a shared latent reasoning space.
llm_ranked.result_en: The shared latent space enables robust transfer from human demonstrations to robot policies, improving generalization across different embodiments.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: LaST-HD shows that aligning latent physical dynamics enables effective imitation learning from human demonstrations for robotics.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: imitation learning from human demonstrations for robots

llm_ranked.evidence_en: Proposes flow-based manipulation using probability velocity fields as embodiment-agnostic representation for foundation models
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: Proposes flow-based manipulation using probability velocity fields as embodiment-agnostic representation for foundation models
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Cross-embodiment data is key for robotic foundation models but requires embodiment-agnostic motion representations. This paper models robot flows as probability velocity fields via flow matching, treating them as dense continuous-time motions rather than sparse keypoints. The framework generates high-quality robot flow fields for manipulation tasks, demonstrating that probability flows are an effective representation for heterogeneous robot data. This work directly contributes to building generalist robot foundation models by providing a unified motion representation.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Robotic foundation models need embodiment-agnostic motion representations; existing flow-based methods use sparse keypoints instead of dense velocity fields.
llm_ranked.method_en: Proposes Flow as Flow, a framework that models robot velocity fields as probability flows using a flow matching formulation to generate dense continuous-time motions.
llm_ranked.result_en: The method achieves efficient and high-quality robot flow generation for object manipulation, outperforming keypoint-based baselines.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Probability velocity fields serve as an effective embodiment-agnostic representation, advancing the development of generalist robot foundation models.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: robot foundation model for generalist robotics

llm_ranked.evidence_en: Addresses sim-to-real challenges for robot navigation on sidewalks via monocular 3D occupancy perception
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: Addresses sim-to-real challenges for robot navigation on sidewalks via monocular 3D occupancy perception
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Sim-to-real transfer for robot navigation on sidewalks faces challenges due to cluttered and unstructured environments. This paper proposes WalkOCC, a hybrid 2D-3D monocular 3D occupancy perception framework that couples geometric grounding from LiDAR-RGB paired data with scalable learning from large-scale unpaired data. The method explicitly tackles sidewalk-specific characteristics and enables robust occupancy prediction from a single camera, facilitating safer navigation for delivery bots and wheelchairs. It directly addresses sim-to-real gaps in mobile robot navigation.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Existing occupancy learning is designed for on-road driving and does not capture sidewalk-specific challenges like clutter and less structured scenes.
llm_ranked.method_en: Proposes WalkOCC, a hybrid ray-marching monocular 3D occupancy perception framework that combines geometric grounding from paired data with scalable learning from large-scale unpaired data.
llm_ranked.result_en: WalkOCC achieves accurate 3D occupancy prediction on sidewalk environments from monocular input, outperforming methods relying on multiple cameras or LiDAR.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: The work provides a practical solution for sim-to-real navigation transfer for robots operating on sidewalks, enhancing safety and robustness.

llm_ranked.evidence_en: Proposes online adaptation for VLA models using advantage-guided self-distillation
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: Proposes online adaptation for VLA models using advantage-guided self-distillation
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Online adaptation of VLA models faces challenges due to sparse reward signals. This paper introduces ROAD-VLA, an advantage-guided self-distillation framework that creates a proximal teacher in action space by perturbing action-token logits with calibrated advantage estimates. This converts sparse rewards into dense token-level learning signals, enabling effective online fine-tuning of VLA policies without privileged information. Results show improved adaptation in manipulation tasks, advancing vision-language-action models for robot control in dynamic settings.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Online adaptation of VLA models is difficult due to sparse rewards and a modality gap between symbolic guidance and low-level actions.
llm_ranked.method_en: Proposes ROAD-VLA, which constructs a proximal teacher by perturbing action-token logits with advantage estimates, enabling dense token-level self-distillation.
llm_ranked.result_en: ROAD-VLA improves online adaptation performance on robotic manipulation tasks, outperforming text-based privileged teachers and standard fine-tuning.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: The framework enables robust online adaptation of VLA models without requiring privileged information, enhancing their applicability.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: vision language action model for robot control

llm_ranked.evidence_en: Develops a real-to-sim-to-real platform for household mobile manipulation addressing sim-to-real transfer
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: Develops a real-to-sim-to-real platform for household mobile manipulation addressing sim-to-real transfer
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Sim-to-real transfer for household mobile manipulation faces challenges in scene reconstruction, strategy evaluation, and deployment. This paper presents BestMan, a scalable platform that enables seamless real-to-sim-to-real transfer by reconstructing high-fidelity simulation scenes, evaluating strategies systematically, and deploying policies in the real world. The platform bridges the simulation-real gap, allowing effective training and transfer of manipulation policies. It directly addresses sim-to-real challenges for robot navigation and manipulation in unstructured home environments.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Achieving seamless real-to-sim-to-real transfer for mobile manipulation is hindered by costly reconstruction, complex evaluation, and incompatible deployment.
llm_ranked.method_en: Develops BestMan, a scalable platform that integrates scene reconstruction, strategy evaluation, and real deployment for household mobile manipulation.
llm_ranked.result_en: BestMan enables effective transfer of manipulation policies from simulation to real-world household environments, improving task success rates.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: The platform provides a practical solution for sim-to-real transfer in mobile manipulation, accelerating robotic deployment.

llm_ranked.evidence_en: Deep reinforcement learning for long-range navigation of a legged-wheeled robot in real environments
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: Deep reinforcement learning for long-range navigation of a legged-wheeled robot in real environments
llm_ranked.tldr_en: This paper presents a deep reinforcement learning based locomotion controller and navigation system for the legged-wheeled robot Go2-W. The controller is extended from a proprioception-only policy previously used for quadruped robots. The system is applied to long-range autonomous navigation in real outdoor environments. Results demonstrate successful navigation over long distances, combining efficient flat-ground mobility with rough-terrain capability. This work advances DRL for real-world robot navigation.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Long-range autonomous navigation of legged-wheeled robots in real environments remains limited despite their potential.
llm_ranked.method_en: A DRL-based locomotion controller is extended to the 16-DoF legged-wheeled robot Go2-W and integrated with an autonomous navigation system.
llm_ranked.result_en: The robot successfully performs long-distance autonomous navigation in real-world environments, demonstrating robustness.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: DRL enables effective real-world navigation for legged-wheeled robots, combining mobility and autonomy.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: reinforcement learning for robot control

llm_ranked.evidence_en: Proposes a world action model for generalizable manipulation across diverse environments
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: Proposes a world action model for generalizable manipulation across diverse environments
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Generalizable manipulation across environments remains challenging. MV-WAM is an end-to-end framework that jointly models visual prediction, action generation, and value estimation, leveraging video priors to enhance action generalization under distribution shift. It addresses structural mismatch between visual and action modalities via manifold-aware design. Experiments show improved out-of-distribution performance. The method contributes to large-scale robot learning for manipulation.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: World action models show limited out-of-distribution generalization due to a structural mismatch between visual and action modalities.
llm_ranked.method_en: MV-WAM jointly models visual prediction, action generation, and value estimation with manifold-aware learning and video priors.
llm_ranked.result_en: MV-WAM achieves better action generalization on out-of-distribution manipulation scenarios compared to prior world action models.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: MV-WAM advances generalizable robot manipulation by addressing modality mismatch and leveraging video priors for robust action generation.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: Large scale robot learning techniques for robotic manipulation tasks

llm_ranked.evidence_en: Addresses local navigation and obstacle avoidance for humanoid robots with limited perception
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: Addresses local navigation and obstacle avoidance for humanoid robots with limited perception
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Humanoid navigation in cluttered environments requires joint obstacle avoidance, goal recovery, and stable locomotion under limited sensing. LP-NavOA is a limited-perception framework using raycast-conditioned PPO for locomotion and a safety filter. It integrates navigation and control to handle sparse goals and occlusion. Results show improved navigation success in challenging conditions. This work directly tackles sim-to-real challenges for robot navigation.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Humanoid local navigation under limited perception struggles with joint obstacle avoidance and goal recovery.
llm_ranked.method_en: LP-NavOA uses raycast-conditioned PPO with a shared safety filter for integrated navigation and obstacle avoidance.
llm_ranked.result_en: LP-NavOA demonstrates robust local navigation and obstacle avoidance on humanoid robots under limited perception.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: LP-NavOA provides an effective integrated approach for humanoid navigation, addressing key challenges in sim-to-real transfer.

llm_ranked.evidence_en: Proposes a framework for imitation learning of object interactions using manipulation modes
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: Proposes a framework for imitation learning of object interactions using manipulation modes
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Imitation learning for object interaction tasks often lacks interpretability. RE4 revisits modern IL benchmarks and repurposes principled manipulation theories to build a framework that preserves both performance and interpretability. It uses lightweight self-supervised pose estimation from demonstrations and integrates transformation-aware imitation. Results show competitive performance with end-to-end methods while offering interpretability. This work advances imitation learning from human demonstrations for manipulation.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: End-to-end imitation learning sacrifices interpretability; a principled framework is needed for object interactions.
llm_ranked.method_en: RE4 combines model-free pose estimation and manipulation mode theory for transformation-aware imitation learning.
llm_ranked.result_en: RE4 matches performance of diffusion/flow methods while providing interpretable manipulation modes on object interaction benchmarks.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: RE4 demonstrates that principled manipulation theories can yield both high performance and interpretability in imitation learning.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: imitation learning from human demonstrations for robots

llm_ranked.evidence_en: Proposes pretraining action module with motion priors for cross-embodiment VLA models
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: Proposes pretraining action module with motion priors for cross-embodiment VLA models
llm_ranked.tldr_en: VLA models lack explicit motion priors, hindering cross-embodiment learning. This work introduces a two-stage training framework that pretrains the action module with cross-embodiment temporal motion structure before VLA alignment. This equips the action module with motion priors independent of specific embodiments. Experiments show improved cross-embodiment transfer and sample efficiency. The method directly enhances VLA models for generalist robot control.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: VLA models learn action from scratch, lacking motion priors for efficient cross-embodiment learning.
llm_ranked.method_en: The framework first pretrains the action module with temporal motion structure, then performs cross-modal VLA alignment.
llm_ranked.result_en: The approach improves cross-embodiment manipulation performance and reduces training requirements.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Pretraining action priors enables more effective cross-embodiment VLA policies for generalist robotics.

llm_ranked.evidence_en: Introduces a training recipe for zero-shot cross-embodiment VLA manipulation by masking end-effector
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: Introduces a training recipe for zero-shot cross-embodiment VLA manipulation by masking end-effector
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Cross-embodiment transfer for VLA models typically requires data from new embodiments. Cloak proposes masking the end-effector from the wrist camera view to make visual reasoning embodiment-agnostic. It renders a mask using robot geometry in simulation and augments during training. Cloak-VLA trained only on parallel-jaw gripper data transfers zero-shot to new embodiments. Results show successful manipulation on unseen grippers. This advances generalist VLA policies.

llm_ranked.method_en: Cloak masks the end-effector in wrist camera views during training to achieve embodiment-agnostic visual reasoning for VLA.
llm_ranked.result_en: Cloak-VLA achieves zero-shot cross-embodiment manipulation without data from target embodiments.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Cloak provides a simple yet effective recipe for generalist VLA manipulation across embodiments.

llm_ranked.evidence_en: Proposes a data augmentation framework for VLA policies using failure-driven object swapping
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: Proposes a data augmentation framework for VLA policies using failure-driven object swapping
llm_ranked.tldr_en: VLA policies fail on out-of-distribution objects, but collecting new data is costly. Pose6DAug is a data augmentation framework that converts successful episodes into targeted demonstrations for failure modes without new data collection. It swaps manipulated objects while preserving physical validity using multi-view observations and calibration. Experiments show improved generalization to novel objects. This enables large-scale robot learning with diverse data.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: VLA policies need diverse object data but collecting demonstrations for every failure case is expensive.
llm_ranked.method_en: Pose6DAug swaps objects in successful episodes using calibrated multi-view data to generate augmented demonstrations for failures.
llm_ranked.result_en: Pose6DAug improves VLA policy generalization to novel objects without additional data collection.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Pose6DAug offers a cost-effective data augmentation to scale robot learning with diverse object appearances.

llm_ranked.evidence_en: modular framework for zero-shot language-guided robotic manipulation using VLM and LLM
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: modular framework for zero-shot language-guided robotic manipulation using VLM and LLM
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Standard VLMs suffer from spatial ambiguity in robotic manipulation. This paper presents a modular training-free framework that decomposes the vision-action pipeline into perception, semantic interpretation, and execution. It uses FastSAM and SoM prompting for grounded visual anchors and an LLM as a semantic router. The approach enables zero-shot language-guided manipulation in semi-structured environments without fine-tuning, bridging high-level reasoning and low-level kinematics.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: To overcome spatial ambiguity and semantic hallucinations in VLMs for zero-shot robotic manipulation.
llm_ranked.method_en: Modular framework using FastSAM and Set-of-Mark prompting for visual grounding, then an LLM as semantic router for task execution.
llm_ranked.result_en: The framework achieves zero-shot manipulation in semi-structured environments with natural language instructions.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: A modular VLM-LLM pipeline can perform robust zero-shot manipulation without fine-tuning.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: vision language action model for robot control

llm_ranked.evidence_en: memory retrieval in visuomotor policies via imitation learning for long-horizon control
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: memory retrieval in visuomotor policies via imitation learning for long-horizon control
llm_ranked.tldr_en: General-purpose robots need memory for long-horizon tasks, but naive integration into imitation learning causes spurious correlations. This paper proposes a memory retrieval mechanism using transformer attention over long contexts, learned from offline data. It addresses two challenges: avoiding spurious correlations and handling variable history lengths. Experiments show improved success rates on long-horizon tasks in simulated and real environments, enabling robots to recall past events.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: To enable memory in visuomotor policies for long-horizon robot control without task-specific rules.
llm_ranked.method_en: Transformer-based memory retrieval mechanism integrated into imitation learning from offline data, addressing spurious correlations.
llm_ranked.result_en: The method improves long-horizon task success in both simulated and real robot experiments.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Learned memory retrieval from offline data is effective for long-horizon robot control.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: imitation learning from human demonstrations for robots

llm_ranked.evidence_en: neural action codec for vision-language-action models enabling efficient action tokenization
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: neural action codec for vision-language-action models enabling efficient action tokenization
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Vision-language-action (VLA) models rely on discrete action tokenizers that trade off compression and latency. NAC treats robot action trajectories as multi-channel 1D signals and compresses them with a multi-scale RVQGAN architecture inspired by neural audio codecs. Experiments show improved downstream performance and reduced latency for robot control. This work provides a new perspective on action representation for VLA models.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Existing action tokenizers for VLA models have suboptimal compression-latency trade-offs.
llm_ranked.method_en: NAC applies a multi-scale residual vector quantization generative adversarial network to action trajectories as signals.
llm_ranked.result_en: NAC achieves better compression and downstream performance compared to prior tokenizers.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Audio-inspired neural codec design is effective for action tokenization in VLA models, enhancing efficiency.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: vision language action model for robot control

llm_ranked.evidence_en: delay-aware adapter for smooth asynchronous control in vision-language-action models
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: delay-aware adapter for smooth asynchronous control in vision-language-action models
llm_ranked.tldr_en: VLA models suffer from inference latency causing action jitter in asynchronous execution. Action ControlNet (ACNet) is a lightweight delay-aware adapter that mitigates handoff discontinuities without full-policy retraining. Experiments on contact-rich manipulation tasks show smoother control and improved success rates. This work provides a practical solution to a key deployment challenge for VLA models.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Asynchronous execution in VLA models leads to action discontinuities from stale observations.
llm_ranked.method_en: ACNet adds a lightweight delay-aware adapter to existing VLA models.
llm_ranked.result_en: Smoother control and improved performance in contact-rich manipulation tasks.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Lightweight adapters can effectively address control latency in VLA models, enabling stable real-time control.

llm_ranked.evidence_en: exploration technique for sample efficient finetuning in RL
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: exploration technique for sample efficient finetuning in RL
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Improving sample efficiency of finetuning pretrained generative control policies online. Proposes DF-ExpEnse, an exploration technique that uses the generative policy's multimodal capabilities to create a candidate set, then uses an ensemble of critics to select actions balancing quality and exploration. Fleet settings enable cross-agent communication for collaborative exploration. Improves online experience collection quality.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Need better exploration for sample-efficient finetuning of pretrained robot policies.
llm_ranked.method_en: DF-ExpEnse uses diffusion-filtered exploration with ensemble critics for action selection.
llm_ranked.result_en: Improves quality of online experience collection, increasing finetuning sample-efficiency.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: DF-ExpEnse enhances finetuning by leveraging generative policies for exploration.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: reinforcement learning for robot control

llm_ranked.evidence_en: trajectory standardization for imitation learning from human demos
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: trajectory standardization for imitation learning from human demos
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Human demonstration trajectories for imitation learning are noisy and temporally irregular. This paper proposes Information-Standardized Trajectory Resampling (ISR), an offline preprocessing method that resamples trajectories to enforce equal information distance between points. ISR removes speed non-uniformity and redundant pauses, improving data quality and policy learning. Demonstrated on robotic manipulation tasks.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Noisy and irregular human demonstration trajectories degrade imitation learning quality.
llm_ranked.method_en: ISR resamples trajectories to equalize information distance, removing speed-induced non-uniformity.
llm_ranked.result_en: Improves data quality and policy learning for robotic manipulation imitation learning.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Trajectory standardization is an effective preprocessing step for imitation learning.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: imitation learning from human demonstrations for robots

llm_ranked.evidence_en: active continual learning for vision-language-action models
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: active continual learning for vision-language-action models
llm_ranked.tldr_en: VLA models are typically fine-tuned passively, requiring failure before data collection. RECALL introduces an active, uncertainty-guided data collection paradigm that efficiently selects states needing supervision. Experiments show it achieves more efficient fine-tuning than passive collection. This reduces demonstrator effort and improves learning efficiency in continual learning settings.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Passive imitation learning for VLAs wastes effort on states where the policy already performs well.
llm_ranked.method_en: RECALL uses uncertainty-guided active data collection to select informative states for fine-tuning.
llm_ranked.result_en: Active data collection leads to more efficient fine-tuning compared to passive demonstrations.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: This work enables efficient continual learning for VLA models by focusing human effort on critical states.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: vision language action model for robot control

llm_ranked.evidence_en: future-oriented conditioning for data-efficient VLA adaptation
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: future-oriented conditioning for data-efficient VLA adaptation
llm_ranked.tldr_en: VLA models underperform in few-shot settings. FOCA combines explicit prediction of future interaction embeddings with implicit goal alignment to enable long-horizon reasoning in latent space. It eliminates the need for pixel-level prediction. Experiments show improved data efficiency on robotic manipulation tasks, making VLA adaptation practical with limited demonstrations.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: VLA models degrade sharply under few-shot imitation learning, limiting practical adoption.
llm_ranked.method_en: FOCA uses future-oriented conditioning with explicit future embeddings and implicit goal alignment.
llm_ranked.result_en: The method improves data efficiency on robotic manipulation tasks in few-shot settings.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: FOCA enables effective VLA adaptation with limited demonstrations through long-horizon latent reasoning.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: vision language action model for robot control

llm_ranked.evidence_en: VLA model for whole-body humanoid locomotion
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: VLA model for whole-body humanoid locomotion
llm_ranked.tldr_en: WOLF-VLA addresses the challenge of applying Vision-Language-Action models to whole-body humanoid locomotion. It combines whole-body optimal-control motion synthesis with a large-scale multi-modal dataset. The framework successfully trains VLAs to generate locomotion policies from natural language instructions. This work broadens VLA applicability beyond manipulation to contact-rich locomotion tasks.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: VLA models lack applicability to whole-body humanoid locomotion due to data scarcity and difficulty encoding optimality.
llm_ranked.method_en: Integrates whole-body optimal-control motion synthesis with a large-scale multi-modal dataset to train VLA policies.
llm_ranked.result_en: Constructs comprehensive dataset and enables VLA-based locomotion policies directly from natural language.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Extends VLA models to humanoid locomotion, demonstrating potential for generalist robotics.

llm_ranked.evidence_en: training-free compression for VLA models
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: training-free compression for VLA models
llm_ranked.tldr_en: This paper reveals layer-wise representational redundancy in large Vision-Language-Action models. It proposes a training-free structural compression pipeline to reduce parameters. The method bypasses the need for loading full-scale models during fine-tuning. This achieves computational savings for large VLA models like pi0 and GR00T-N1.5.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Multi-billion parameter VLA models impose prohibitive computational costs for fine-tuning and inference.
llm_ranked.method_en: Introduces a training-free structural compression pipeline exploiting layer redundancy in VLA foundation policies.
llm_ranked.result_en: Achieves compression without full model loading, enabling efficient fine-tuning and inference.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Provides a practical approach to reduce computational burden of large VLA models.

llm_ranked.evidence_en: reactive action chunking with world model for VLA policies
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: reactive action chunking with world model for VLA policies
llm_ranked.tldr_en: DREAM-Chunk augments chunking-based Vision-Language-Action policies with a lightweight latent world model at test time. It samples multiple candidate action chunks and selects those whose predicted futures match actual rollouts. The method requires no additional fine-tuning. It addresses brittleness of open-loop action chunk execution in stochastic environments.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Open-loop action chunk execution is brittle under stochastic dynamics and partial observability.
llm_ranked.method_en: Adds a latent world model at test time to select actions from chunk with best predicted state matching.
llm_ranked.result_en: Improves robustness of VLA policies without extra fine-tuning.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Enhances VLA policy reliability via test-time scaling.

llm_ranked.evidence_en: self-improvement framework for dexterous manipulation
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: self-improvement framework for dexterous manipulation
llm_ranked.tldr_en: ENPIRE introduces a harness framework for coding agents to automate robot policy improvement in the real world. It instantiates a physical feedback loop with modules for automatic reset, execution, verification, and refinement. The goal is to reduce human supervision and algorithm engineering. It targets dexterous manipulation tasks.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Dexterous manipulation relies heavily on human supervision and algorithm engineering.
llm_ranked.method_en: A repeatable feedback loop with four modules: Environment, Policy, Verified, and Improvement.
llm_ranked.result_en: Enables automated policy self-improvement in real-world settings.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Advances toward general physical intelligence by reducing human bottleneck.

llm_ranked.evidence_en: Wh0 uses generative world models to produce egocentric human hand manipulation data for imitation learning
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: Wh0 uses generative world models to produce egocentric human hand manipulation data for imitation learning
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Scaling dexterous manipulation requires diverse data. Wh0 uses generative video world models to produce controllable egocentric human-hand manipulation data, bypassing costly teleoperation. This data unlocks capabilities in pretrained dexterous VLA models. Wh0 generates a 50k-episode dataset conditioned on language, objects, and scenes.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: To generate scalable and controllable human-hand manipulation data for dexterous VLA models.
llm_ranked.method_en: Wh0 uses a generative world model to produce egocentric human-hand manipulation videos conditioned on language, objects, and scenes.
llm_ranked.result_en: Generates a 50k-episode dataset that improves dexterous VLA model performance.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Generative world models can serve as scalable data sources for robot imitation learning.

llm_ranked.evidence_en: PolicyTrim improves intrinsic policy efficiency of Vision-Language-Action models
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: PolicyTrim improves intrinsic policy efficiency of Vision-Language-Action models
llm_ranked.tldr_en: VLA models suffer from low policy efficiency due to action chunk redundancy and planning unreliability. PolicyTrim analyzes and boosts intrinsic policy efficiency by optimizing action chunk length and reducing redundant steps. Experiments show significant reduction in forward inference calls while maintaining task success.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: To improve the intrinsic policy efficiency of VLA models for real-world deployment.
llm_ranked.method_en: PolicyTrim optimizes action chunk length and reduces redundant actions to minimize inference calls.
llm_ranked.result_en: Reduces total forward inference calls without sacrificing task success.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: PolicyTrim makes VLA models more efficient for real-world robotics.

llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: ASCII Art turns LLMs into VLA controllers by rendering visual observations as ASCII
llm_ranked.tldr_en: VLA controllers typically require multimodal backbones with large compute. This paper shows that a text-only LLM can be adapted into a VLA controller by rendering visual observations as ASCII art. This enables efficient VLA control using existing LLM stacks. Fine-tuning with expert demonstrations and DAgger shows competitive performance.

llm_ranked.method_en: Renders visual observations as ASCII art input to an LLM, which outputs executable actions.
llm_ranked.result_en: Achieves competitive VLA control performance with efficient LLMs.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: ASCII representation can bridge visual and action modalities for LLM-based robot control.

llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: large-scale evaluation of pre-trained ViTs for real-world robot navigation
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Pre-trained visual encoders are critical for robot navigation policies. This large-scale study runs 966 real-world navigation episodes over 24km to evaluate state-of-the-art ViTs. It investigates which pre-training components matter most, providing insights for foundation model evaluation.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: To evaluate which components of pre-trained visual encoders are important for real-world robot navigation.
llm_ranked.method_en: Large-scale real-world navigation experiments comparing different pre-trained ViTs.
llm_ranked.result_en: Identifies key factors in pre-training that affect navigation performance.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Provides practical guidance for selecting visual encoders in robot navigation.

llm_ranked.evidence_en: diffusion model imitation learning for manipulation
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: diffusion model imitation learning for manipulation
llm_ranked.tldr_en: CoRDE addresses structural generalization in multi-task robot imitation learning by proposing a concept-prior routed diffusion experts framework that uses a frozen concept encoder to guide variational distillation, enabling better multi-modal action distribution capture without parameter explosion.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Monolithic diffusion models struggle with gradient conflicts in long-horizon tasks, requiring structural generalization.
llm_ranked.method_en: CoRDE extracts semantic distributions from a frozen concept encoder to guide variational distillation of diffusion experts.
llm_ranked.result_en: The method improves structural generalization in multi-task robot manipulation over baselines.

llm_ranked.evidence_en: dexterous manipulation contact estimation
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: dexterous manipulation contact estimation
llm_ranked.tldr_en: NoContactNoWorries presents a transformer-based multimodal framework that fuses RGB-D vision with proprioception to infer binary contact for in-hand dexterous manipulation, offering a scalable alternative to tactile sensors.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Tactile sensors are costly and fragile; humans infer contact from vision and proprioception.
llm_ranked.method_en: A transformer fuses RGB-D and proprioceptive data to estimate contact.
llm_ranked.result_en: The method achieves accurate contact estimation without tactile sensors.

llm_ranked.evidence_en: reinforcement learning for long-horizon manipulation
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: reinforcement learning for long-horizon manipulation
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Temporal Self-Imitation Learning (TSIL) mines temporally efficient successful trajectories during RL training and uses them as self-supervision to improve long-horizon robot manipulation policies, preventing exploitation of dense rewards.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Dense rewards lead to inefficient behavior and forgetting of efficient ones.
llm_ranked.method_en: Efficiency-weighted self-imitation learning with adaptive temporal targets.
llm_ranked.result_en: Improves policy efficiency and performance in long-horizon tasks.

llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: imitation learning from human demonstrations for dual-robot collaboration
llm_ranked.tldr_en: DUET introduces a dual-robot learning framework for mobile manipulation that uses synchronized VR teleoperation and human-human tracking to collect data efficiently, and an Action Chunking Transformer for pretraining collaborative skills.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Data acquisition is a bottleneck for dual-robot collaboration.
llm_ranked.method_en: Unified dual-embodiment VR teleoperation and human tracking pipeline with ACT architecture.
llm_ranked.result_en: Enables efficient learning of collaborative mobile manipulation.

llm_ranked.evidence_en: reinforcement learning framework with self-imitation for robot control
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: reinforcement learning framework with self-imitation for robot control
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Temporal Self-Imitation Learning (TSIL) addresses the problem of forgetting rare efficient behaviors in long-horizon robot manipulation RL. It mines temporally efficient successful trajectories and uses them as reusable supervision via adaptive temporal targets and efficiency-weighted replay. TSIL improves sample efficiency and final performance on manipulation tasks, demonstrating that temporal efficiency is a powerful self-supervision signal for RL in robotics.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Long-horizon robot manipulation RL suffers from forgetting rare efficient behaviors despite high returns.
llm_ranked.method_en: TSIL mines temporally efficient successful trajectories and uses them to generate adaptive temporal targets for policy improvement with efficiency-weighted replay.
llm_ranked.result_en: Improved sample efficiency and final performance on long-horizon manipulation tasks compared to baselines.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Temporal efficiency is a valuable self-supervision source for reinforcement learning in robotics.

llm_ranked.evidence_en: invertible adapter for one-step flow matching with multimodal inputs for robot manipulation
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: invertible adapter for one-step flow matching with multimodal inputs for robot manipulation
llm_ranked.tldr_en: This paper presents an invertible neural network adapter for one-step flow matching in robotic manipulation, taking visual, linguistic, and proprioceptive inputs to generate high-dimensional actions efficiently. It constrains generation in an invertible latent space, reducing inference steps while maintaining accuracy. Experiments on dexterous manipulation show substantially reduced inference complexity with strong performance, advancing efficient VLA models.

llm_ranked.method_en: The invertible adapter projects multimodal observations into an invertible latent space and performs one-step denoising.
llm_ranked.result_en: Substantially reduced inference complexity with maintained accuracy and stability on dexterous manipulation.
