llm_ranked.evidence_en: human-in-the-loop residual RL adapter for finetuning VLA models
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: human-in-the-loop residual RL adapter for finetuning VLA models
llm_ranked.tldr_en: This paper proposes a plug-and-play fine-tuning pipeline for VLA models using human-in-the-loop residual reinforcement learning. It addresses compounding errors and distributional shift of behavior cloning. The method is model-agnostic and allows robust deployment in real-world environments. Experiments on robot manipulation tasks show improved success rates, enabling practical deployment of VLA models.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: VLA models trained with behavior cloning suffer from distributional shift and compounding errors, limiting deployment.
llm_ranked.method_en: A human-in-the-loop residual RL framework that fine-tunes VLA models without being constrained by specific architectures.
llm_ranked.result_en: Improved robustness and success rate on real-world manipulation tasks.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Human-in-the-loop residual RL is an effective finetuning strategy for VLA models.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: vision language action model for robot control

llm_ranked.evidence_en: Memory-augmented world model for persistent robot manipulation
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: Memory-augmented world model for persistent robot manipulation
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Persistent world modeling in manipulation is challenged by end-effector occlusions and rapid motion causing forgetting or hallucination. Mem-World proposes a memory-augmented multi-view action-conditioned world model that retrieves informative historical observations to predict future views. Results show improved video consistency and reduced hallucination compared to prior world models. This enables better simulation and policy evaluation for manipulation tasks.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Existing action-conditioned world models suffer from forgetting or hallucinating scene details in dynamic manipulation scenarios.
llm_ranked.method_en: Mem-World uses a memory-augmented multi-view action-conditioned world model with selective history retrieval for future frame prediction.
llm_ranked.result_en: Improved video consistency and reduced hallucination in manipulation tasks compared to prior world model approaches.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Memory augmentation enhances persistence of world models for manipulation, enabling more reliable simulation.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: Large scale robot learning techniques for robotic manipulation tasks

llm_ranked.evidence_en: Human demonstration for humanoid loco-manipulation learning
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: Human demonstration for humanoid loco-manipulation learning
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Directly transferring human demonstrations to humanoids is brittle due to tracking errors and human-to-humanoid gaps. HALOMI extends Universal Manipulation Interface (UMI) with egocentric sensing to collect ego-view and wrist-view observations from human demonstrations, then learns a policy for loco-manipulation with active perception. Experiments on a humanoid robot show successful coordinated locomotion and manipulation. This enables scalable imitation learning for humanoid robots.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Transferring human demonstrations to humanoids is challenging due to tracking brittleness and observation-action gaps.
llm_ranked.method_en: HALOMI extends UMI with egocentric sensing and active perception to collect and learn from human demonstrations for whole-body control.
llm_ranked.result_en: Successful coordinated locomotion and manipulation on a humanoid robot from human demonstrations.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Scalable imitation learning framework for humanoid loco-manipulation using human demonstrations.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: imitation learning from human demonstrations for robots

llm_ranked.evidence_en: Progress reward model for RL in robot manipulation
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: Progress reward model for RL in robot manipulation
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Reinforcement learning for manipulation is bottlenecked by reward design, with sparse rewards providing weak supervision. RARM converts a single successful demonstration into a dense progress-aware reward using a lightweight visual comparator. It is trained once and generalizes across tasks without task-specific demonstrations. Experiments on various manipulation tasks show faster learning and higher success rates compared to sparse rewards. This reduces human effort in reward engineering.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Reward design is a major bottleneck in reinforcement learning for robot manipulation tasks.
llm_ranked.method_en: RARM uses a visual comparator trained on a single successful demonstration to generate dense progress-aware rewards for RL.
llm_ranked.result_en: Faster learning and higher success rates across multiple manipulation tasks compared to sparse and handcrafted rewards.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Lightweight reward model from a single demo enables efficient RL for manipulation without manual reward design.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: reinforcement learning for robot control

llm_ranked.evidence_en: Semi-supervised VLA adaptation for robot control
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: Semi-supervised VLA adaptation for robot control
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Adapting Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models to new environments requires costly action-labeled demonstrations. SemiVLA proposes a self-distilled teacher-student framework that learns from action-unlabeled vision-language observations by generating reliable pseudo-actions. Experiments demonstrate competitive performance with fully supervised methods using only a fraction of action labels. This significantly reduces the annotation cost for deploying VLA models in new tasks.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: VLA model adaptation to new environments depends on expensive action-labeled demonstrations.
llm_ranked.method_en: SemiVLA uses a self-distilled teacher-student framework to generate reliable pseudo-actions from unlabeled vision-language data.
llm_ranked.result_en: Competitive performance with fully supervised methods using only a small portion of action labels.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Semi-supervised learning reduces annotation cost for adapting VLA models to new environments.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: vision language action model for robot control

llm_ranked.evidence_en: improving VLA with behavioral memories for long horizon manipulation
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: improving VLA with behavioral memories for long horizon manipulation
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Standard VLA models struggle with partially observable manipulation tasks due to lack of memory. The paper introduces CAMP, a compressed action memory policy that learns a compact representation of past actions. Experiments on long-horizon contact-rich tasks show that CAMP significantly improves success rates over memory-less baselines. This work demonstrates that leveraging action history as a self-supervised signal enables more robust VLA policies for complex manipulation.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Partially observable manipulation tasks challenge VLA models because they lack memory of past actions and context.
llm_ranked.method_en: CAMP trains a memory module to compress the robot's action history into a latent representation that conditions the policy.
llm_ranked.result_en: CAMP outperforms standard visuomotor and VLA policies on long-horizon tasks with partial observability.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Action history provides a strong self-supervised signal for memory in VLA policies, improving robustness in contact-rich manipulation.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: vision language action model for robot control

llm_ranked.evidence_en: VLM-based navigation addressing safety and latency
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: VLM-based navigation addressing safety and latency
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Robot navigation in safety-critical scenarios faces challenges from unforeseen semantic events. Large VLMs cause computational latency. This paper proposes Event-Adaptive Motion Planning (EAMP) with a prompt-configurable semantic event trigger to selectively activate VLM intervention, improving efficiency. Experiments show reduced latency and safe navigation. This work enables practical VLM-based navigation.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Large VLMs introduce latency that destabilizes robot control in safety-critical navigation.
llm_ranked.method_en: Event-Adaptive Motion Planning (EAMP) with a distilled VLM and a prompt-configurable semantic event trigger.
llm_ranked.result_en: Selective VLM activation reduces latency while maintaining safe navigation in dynamic scenarios.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: EAMP provides an efficient framework for VLM-based navigation in safety-critical situations.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: What are current challenges in sim to real transfer for robot navigation?

llm_ranked.evidence_en: Reinforcement learning enhanced control for soft robots
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: Reinforcement learning enhanced control for soft robots
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Soft robots are hard to control due to nonlinear dynamics. Data-enabled predictive control (DeePC) is model-free but computationally heavy. This paper proposes RL-ET-DeePC, where a model-free RL policy determines when to invoke the DeePC optimizer, reducing computational cost. Experiments on a 3D cable-driven soft robotic arm show improved efficiency and control performance. This work combines RL and event-triggered control for real-time soft robot control.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: DeePC for soft robots is computationally demanding for real-time deployment.
llm_ranked.method_en: RL policy decides when to trigger DeePC optimization, forming an adaptive event-triggered framework.
llm_ranked.result_en: RL-ET-DeePC reduces computational load while maintaining control accuracy on a soft arm.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: The RL-enhanced event-triggered method enables efficient real-time control of soft robots.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: reinforcement learning for robot control

llm_ranked.evidence_en: Generalization in robotic manipulation via factor-aware mixture-of-experts
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: Generalization in robotic manipulation via factor-aware mixture-of-experts
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Pretrained encoders with diffusion policies struggle to generalize across varying environments. This paper proposes FAME, a framework with factor-aware mixture-of-experts (MoE) and three-stage training to adapt to lighting and texture variations. Lightweight adapters are trained per factor while the encoder and policy remain frozen. Results show improved generalization without full model retraining. FAME enhances foundation model robustness.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Diffusion policies with pretrained encoders fail to generalize to environmental variations.
llm_ranked.method_en: FAME uses factor-aware MoE with factor-specific adapters and a three-stage training pipeline.
llm_ranked.result_en: FAME achieves better generalization than standard diffusion policies across varied environments.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Factor-aware MoE enables efficient adaptation of manipulation policies to environmental changes.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: robot foundation model for generalist robotics

llm_ranked.evidence_en: Reinforcement learning reward modeling for robot manipulation
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: Reinforcement learning reward modeling for robot manipulation
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Reward design is a bottleneck for RL in long-horizon manipulation. Sparse rewards provide weak supervision, dense rewards are labor-intensive. This paper proposes Reference-Anchored Reward Model (RARM), a visual comparator converting a single demonstration into dense progress rewards. RARM is trained once and generalizes across tasks. Experiments show improved RL efficiency and task success rates on manipulation benchmarks.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Hand-crafted dense rewards for RL in manipulation are tedious and task-specific.
llm_ranked.method_en: RARM uses a single demonstration to produce dense progress-aware rewards via visual comparison.
llm_ranked.result_en: RARM accelerates RL training and achieves higher success rates than sparse or heuristic rewards.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: RARM provides a practical alternative to manual reward design for robot manipulation RL.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: reinforcement learning for robot control

llm_ranked.evidence_en: visual navigation in unknown environments, uncertainty-aware
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: visual navigation in unknown environments, uncertainty-aware
llm_ranked.tldr_en: UNSEEN addresses visual navigation in unknown environments for resource-constrained platforms. It proposes uncertainty-aware navigation via sparse estimation to tightly couple motion and perception, overcoming performance degradation from motion blur and illumination changes. The method improves robustness without multi-modal sensors. This work advances vision-only navigation, a key component for sim-to-real transfer in robot navigation.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Visual navigation degrades under challenging conditions due to neglecting perception-motion coupling; existing methods rely on complex multi-modal sensors.
llm_ranked.method_en: UNSEEN employs uncertainty-aware sparse estimation to propagate uncertainty and tightly couple commanded motion with perception in a vision-only pipeline.
llm_ranked.result_en: The method improves navigation robustness under motion blur, low texture, and illumination changes without multi-modal sensors, validated in unknown environments.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: UNSEEN demonstrates that uncertainty-aware sparse estimation enables robust vision-only navigation, reducing system complexity and deployment cost.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: What are current challenges in sim to real transfer for robot navigation?

llm_ranked.evidence_en: fine-tuned VLM critics for robot manipulation
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: fine-tuned VLM critics for robot manipulation
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Large VLMs have world priors useful as critics for steering robot policies, but struggle with fine-grained visual differences in closed-loop manipulation. Robot Critics fine-tunes a VLM critic using pairwise progress supervision from success/failure rollouts. The critic excels at subtle failure detection and progress reasoning. Additionally, an action-conditioned video model predicts visual effects of candidate actions, enabling the critic to select better actions, improving manipulation success.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Current VLMs cannot judge small visual differences between success and failure in closed-loop robot manipulation.
llm_ranked.method_en: Fine-tune VLM critics with pairwise progress supervision from policy rollouts and use action-conditioned video prediction to evaluate candidate actions.
llm_ranked.result_en: The fine-tuned critic outperforms prior progress reasoning baselines in fine-grained failure detection and improves manipulation success.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Fine-tuning VLMs with pairwise progress supervision enables effective real-time critics for robot manipulation.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: How can vision language action models be used for generalist robot manipulation?

llm_ranked.evidence_en: functional grasping with pose-agnostic canonicalization
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: functional grasping with pose-agnostic canonicalization
llm_ranked.tldr_en: AnyMug addresses functional grasping of mug handles, which requires generalization across diverse object geometries and poses. It proposes a canonicalized visuomotor reinforcement learning framework that transforms observation and action into a canonical frame, reducing sensitivity to object configuration. This enables the policy to generalize to upright and inverted placements. The method improves grasp success on thin handles compared to open-loop grasp detectors and standard RL.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Functional grasping policies must generalize across object geometries and poses; current methods are sensitive to object configuration, limiting generalization.
llm_ranked.method_en: AnyMug uses observation-action canonicalization in RL to transform visual observations and actions into a canonical frame, reducing pose variation.
llm_ranked.result_en: The canonicalized RL policy achieves higher grasp success on mug handles across diverse poses, including thin handles and inverted placements.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Canonicalization of observation-action spaces enables robust functional grasping across object configurations.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: robot manipulation with dexterous hands

llm_ranked.evidence_en: RL for soft robot manipulation with novel simulator
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: RL for soft robot manipulation with novel simulator
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Learning contact-rich manipulation for tendon-driven continuum robots is hindered by lack of simulation infrastructure. This work bridges the gap by deriving a continuum-mechanics-informed discretization that places soft robots natively in MuJoCo. It unifies tendon forces, body contact, and dynamics in a single physics pipeline. The simulator is validated against a real robot, enabling RL for soft robot manipulation. This provides a key tool for reinforcement learning and sim-to-real transfer in soft robotics.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Existing soft robot simulators lack contact handling and learning integration, while rigid-body approximations sacrifice physical accuracy for soft robots.
llm_ranked.method_en: Derive a continuum-mechanics-informed discretization to integrate tendon-driven continuum robots into MuJoCo, unifying dynamics and contact.
llm_ranked.result_en: The simulator accurately models soft robot dynamics and contact, enabling successful RL-based manipulation policies that transfer to a real robot.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: This simulation infrastructure enables reinforcement learning and sim-to-real transfer for soft continuum robot manipulation.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: reinforcement learning for robot control

llm_ranked.evidence_en: Develops temporal logic guidance for action-only diffusion policies with world models for robot control
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: Develops temporal logic guidance for action-only diffusion policies with world models for robot control
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Diffusion policies enable multimodal behavior but lack controllability at inference time. This paper proposes a guidance method for action-only diffusion policies that uses a learned world model to enable differentiable evaluation of Signal Temporal Logic (STL) robustness. The gradient of robustness is injected into the diffusion process to steer behavior without jointly generating states. This approach reduces complexity and runtime while providing explicit control, relevant to robot policy foundations and sim-to-real evaluation by enabling safer behavior selection.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Action-only diffusion policies lack control over behavior modes at inference time, and prior STL guidance methods require state generation, increasing complexity.
llm_ranked.method_en: Proposes guiding action-only diffusion policies by injecting gradients from STL robustness computed via a separate learned world model into the diffusion process.
llm_ranked.result_en: The method achieves effective behavioral guidance on robot manipulation tasks with lower runtime compared to state-joint approaches.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: This framework adds control to diffusion policies, improving safety and interpretability for robot control.

llm_ranked.evidence_en: Extends VLA models to sign language input for accessible robot manipulation
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: Extends VLA models to sign language input for accessible robot manipulation
llm_ranked.tldr_en: VLA models for robot manipulation typically rely on speech or text input, limiting accessibility. This paper presents SignVLA, a framework that converts sign language gestures into semantic instructions using an attention-enhanced LSTM on hand landmarks, feeding into downstream VLA policies. The system enables real-time sign language-guided manipulation, demonstrating a novel modality for human-robot interaction. This directly applies VLA models to manipulation tasks, addressing requirement for dexterous hand-based interaction.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Existing VLA interfaces are inaccessible to deaf and speech-impaired users as they rely on speech or text input.
llm_ranked.method_en: Proposes SignVLA with a modular sign-to-text interface using attention LSTM to convert hand landmark features into semantic instructions for VLA policies.
llm_ranked.result_en: SignVLA achieves real-time sign language-guided manipulation, enabling users to control robots via gestures with high accuracy.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: The work broadens accessibility of VLA models and demonstrates flexible input modality for robot manipulation.

llm_ranked.evidence_en: Vision-language-action model for driving with BEV spatial grounding
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: Vision-language-action model for driving with BEV spatial grounding
llm_ranked.tldr_en: DriveStack-VLA addresses the lack of spatial intelligence in VLA driving models. It proposes a framework with a large VLM backbone enhanced by Render-Teacher alignment to produce BEV representations. The method improves geometric modeling and perceptual coverage for precise motion planning. Experiments show that the approach outperforms existing VLA driving models in closed-loop simulations. This work strengthens the spatial grounding of VLA models for autonomous driving.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Existing VLA driving models lack driving-oriented spatial intelligence due to reliance on perspective images and language priors.
llm_ranked.method_en: DriveStack-VLA uses Render-Teacher alignment to produce BEV representations from a large VLM backbone, improving metric geometry and scene structure perception.
llm_ranked.result_en: The model shows improved motion planning accuracy and safety in closed-loop driving simulations.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Strengthening spatial grounding via BEV representations enhances VLA driving models performance and safety.

llm_ranked.evidence_en: memory-efficient policy libraries with low-rank adaptation in reinforcement learning for robotics
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: memory-efficient policy libraries with low-rank adaptation in reinforcement learning for robotics
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning (LoRA) is adapted from LLMs to reinforcement learning for robotics to create memory-efficient policy libraries. Using PPO as base algorithm, LoRA fine-tunes a shared baseline to multiple tasks, reducing memory usage and computational cost. Results demonstrate effective multi-task policy learning with reduced resource requirements. This approach enables scalable multi-task robotics through efficient fine-tuning.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Multi-task robotics requires memory-efficient storage of specialist policies.
llm_ranked.method_en: Apply LoRA to fine-tune a baseline policy for each task using PPO.
llm_ranked.result_en: LoRA-based fine-tuning reduces memory and computation while maintaining performance across tasks.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning is transferable to RL for robotics, enabling scalable policy libraries.

llm_ranked.evidence_en: self-directed play for generalist robot skill acquisition
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: self-directed play for generalist robot skill acquisition
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Current agentic robot systems acquire skills only after explicit instructions. This work introduces Playful Agentic Robot Learning, where an embodied coding agent uses self-directed play for continual skill acquisition before downstream tasks. The RATs framework proposes exploratory tasks, executes policies, diagnoses failures, and distills successful executions into a persistent code skill library. Enables reusable skill acquisition without explicit task instructions.

llm_ranked.method_en: Proposes RATs framework for self-directed play-time skill acquisition using code-as-policy.
llm_ranked.result_en: RATs distills successful executions into a persistent code skill library for reuse.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Self-directed play enables continual skill learning for generalist robot systems.

llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: visual crowd navigation with intention-aware scene representations
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Robot crowd navigation requires inferring human intentions and environmental constraints. Most methods use limited scene representations ignoring rich visual cues. iCrowdNav is a visual crowd navigation method with intention-aware scene representations that encode behavioral and structural context from egocentric observations. Uses spatio-temporal encoder and deep RL to navigate crowds effectively.

llm_ranked.method_en: iCrowdNav employs intention-aware scene representations with spatio-temporal encoders for visual crowd navigation.

llm_ranked.evidence_en: fast safe RL benchmarking for robotics
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: fast safe RL benchmarking for robotics
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Safety is crucial for RL deployment in robotics, but existing safety benchmarks are slow. CRAX is a fast safe RL benchmark built on MuJoCo XLA, achieving ~100x speedup via vectorization and hardware acceleration. It features six environment suites and three agent tasks across three difficulty levels. Evaluates six safe RL algorithms, enabling rapid prototyping.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Existing safety RL benchmarks are computationally slow, limiting experimentation.
llm_ranked.method_en: CRAX leverages MJX and vectorization for fast safe RL benchmarking.
llm_ranked.result_en: Achieves up to 100x speedup over CPU-based benchmarks.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: CRAX enables large-scale safe RL experimentation for robotics and autonomous driving.

llm_ranked.evidence_en: imitation learning with active viewpoint selection
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: imitation learning with active viewpoint selection
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Imitation learning often assumes full observability, but occlusions are common. See2Act uses diffusion models to jointly learn action prediction and active viewpoint selection from keyframe-anchored camera poses. Experiments on Ravens show it recovers informative viewpoints under severe occlusions and improves task success. This enables robots to learn where to look while learning how to act from limited demonstrations.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Standard imitation learning struggles with occlusions; robots need to both search and act.
llm_ranked.method_en: See2Act conditions action prediction on actively inferred viewpoints by coupling action denoising with viewpoint refinement.
llm_ranked.result_en: The policy recovers informative viewpoints under severe occlusions and improves task success in Ravens.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: This work demonstrates that robots can implicitly learn where to see while learning how to act from demonstrations.

llm_ranked.evidence_en: improving imitation learning data augmentation
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: improving imitation learning data augmentation
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Trajectory-level data augmentation for imitation learning often inserts non-expert interpolations that degrade quality. MinInter selects source demonstrations requiring minimal interpolation for each initial configuration, preserving expert behavior. Experiments show it generates higher-quality augmented data, improving policy performance. This reduces the need for large demonstration datasets.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Data augmentation for imitation learning often introduces low-quality non-expert interpolations.
llm_ranked.method_en: MinInter chooses the source demonstration with least interpolation needed to form a complete trajectory.
llm_ranked.result_en: The method improves the quality of augmented data and policy performance in imitation learning tasks.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: MinInter enables efficient use of demonstrations by minimizing non-expert transitions during augmentation.

llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: MemoryWAM introduces persistent memory to world action models for robotic manipulation

llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: Mem-World proposes a memory-augmented action-conditioned world model for persistent robot manipulation
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Persistent world modeling is challenging in manipulation due to occlusions and rapid motion. llm_ranked.evidence_en: metric for trajectory diversity in imitation learning
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: metric for trajectory diversity in imitation learning
llm_ranked.tldr_en: This paper studies how trajectory shape diversity in demonstrations affects imitation learning (IL). It introduces Geometric Entropy (H_G), a task-agnostic metric that quantifies intrinsic diversity after normalizing extrinsic variation. Experiments on contact-rich manipulation show an inverted-U relationship: moderate diversity helps, but too much harms performance, with optimal shifts toward lower diversity for harder tasks. This metric guides data collection and selection for IL.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Understanding how trajectory diversity in demonstrations affects IL performance is crucial for efficient data collection.
llm_ranked.method_en: Geometric Entropy metric normalizes away extrinsic variation via target-frame alignment and measures intrinsic trajectory shape diversity.
llm_ranked.result_en: Observed inverted-U relationship between diversity and success; optimal entropy depends on task difficulty.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: The metric can guide data selection to improve imitation learning in robotics.

llm_ranked.evidence_en: flow matching policy with adaptive prior distributions for robot manipulation
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: flow matching policy with adaptive prior distributions for robot manipulation
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Latent Action Guided Flow Matching (LAFM) addresses the structural mismatch in flow matching for behavior cloning caused by fixed isotropic priors. It replaces the monolithic Gaussian with an adaptive library of learned prior distributions grounded in a latent action space. Experiments on robot manipulation tasks show improved training efficiency and policy performance, enhancing behavior cloning for robotics.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Current flow matching policies for behavior cloning use a fixed prior that mismatches the fragmented structure of robotic action spaces.
llm_ranked.method_en: LAFM introduces an adaptive library of learned prior distributions conditioned on latent actions to replace the fixed Gaussian.
llm_ranked.result_en: Improved training efficiency and policy performance on manipulation benchmarks.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Adaptive priors better capture action space structure, improving flow matching for robotics.

llm_ranked.evidence_en: simulator-in-the-loop refinement for real-world cloth manipulation from single RGB
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: simulator-in-the-loop refinement for real-world cloth manipulation from single RGB
llm_ranked.tldr_en: This paper brings simulator-in-the-loop optimization to cloth manipulation, using a deformable-object simulator (FLASH) for inference-time refinement from a single RGB input. It includes a real-to-sim module trained purely on synthetic data. Experiments show robust real-world cloth manipulation, bridging the sim-to-real gap and extending simulation-based refinement to deformable objects.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Simulator-in-the-loop optimization has been effective for rigid-body manipulation but not for deformable objects like cloth.
llm_ranked.method_en: A pipeline with synthetic data generation, real-to-sim module, and FLASH simulator for online refinement from single RGB.
llm_ranked.result_en: Successful real-world cloth manipulation from a single RGB image.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Simulator-in-the-loop refinement can be extended to deformable object manipulation.

llm_ranked.evidence_en: Grasp prediction for mobile manipulation of dynamic objects
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: Grasp prediction for mobile manipulation of dynamic objects
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Mobile manipulation of dynamic objects requires coordination of base and arm while adapting to evolving target poses. DynaMOMA couples an anchor-based diffusion model for instantaneous grasp trajectory prediction with a whole-body control policy. Experiments on dynamic grasping tasks show successful grasping of moving objects. This enables robots to interact with dynamic environments for manipulation.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Mobile manipulation of dynamic objects is highly challenging due to continuously evolving target poses and temporal consistency requirements.
llm_ranked.method_en: DynaMOMA uses an anchor-based diffusion model to predict short-horizon grasp trajectories coupled with whole-body control.
llm_ranked.result_en: Successful grasping of moving objects in dynamic scenarios.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Framework enables effective mobile manipulation of dynamic objects.

llm_ranked.evidence_en: zero-shot manipulation transfer via geometric affinity for generalist robotics
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: zero-shot manipulation transfer via geometric affinity for generalist robotics
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Generalizing manipulation to unseen objects typically requires many demonstrations. GRAFT proposes a geometry-aware framework that transfers affordances from a single demonstration using part-based graphs. It retrieves functionally and geometrically similar objects and matches part-level and vertex-level descriptors for contact point transfer. Experiments show zero-shot success on diverse objects, enabling fast adaptation for generalist robotics.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Existing affordance transfer methods neglect geometric similarity crucial for manipulation, limiting zero-shot generalization.
llm_ranked.method_en: GRAFT represents objects as part-based graphs with part and vertex descriptors for retrieval and fine-grained contact matching.
llm_ranked.result_en: GRAFT achieves zero-shot manipulation transfer on unseen objects using only one demonstration per object.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Geometry-aware correspondence enables effective few-shot generalization, key for building generalist robot manipulators.

llm_ranked.evidence_en: fast safe RL benchmarking for robotics with JAX acceleration
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: fast safe RL benchmarking for robotics with JAX acceleration
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Safe reinforcement learning benchmarks with realistic physics are computationally slow. CRAX leverages MuJoCo XLA and vectorization to achieve up to 100x speedup over CPU-based benchmarks. It includes six environment suites and three agent tasks with multiple difficulty levels. This enables rapid prototyping and large-scale experimentation for safe RL in robotics.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Existing safe RL benchmarks are too slow for large-scale experimentation and rapid prototyping.
llm_ranked.method_en: CRAX uses JAX and MJX to accelerate safe RL environments with hardware acceleration and vectorized operations.
llm_ranked.result_en: CRAX achieves up to 100x speedup while maintaining realistic 3D dynamics, enabling faster safe RL research.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Fast safe RL benchmarks are crucial for deploying RL in real-world robotics applications.

llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: Structured scene representations for embodied reasoning in manipulation
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Embodied reasoning under real-world variability remains challenging due to demonstration-driven biases. This paper introduces RoBoSR, an intermediate structural representation using object-centric scene graphs for manipulation. It models step-wise state transitions, enabling structured reasoning over preconditions and goals. The approach disentangles high-level reasoning from raw inputs, improving flexibility in long-horizon tasks. Results show enhanced causal reasoning and generalization.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Existing methods lack structured reasoning for open-ended long-horizon manipulation tasks.
llm_ranked.method_en: RoBoSR uses semantically grounded object-centric scene graphs to represent task state transitions.
llm_ranked.result_en: The representation enables causal reasoning and improves performance in long-horizon tasks.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: RoBoSR provides a structured perception-action interface for robust embodied reasoning.

llm_ranked.evidence_en: Local planning for quadruped navigation with collision awareness
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: Local planning for quadruped navigation with collision awareness
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Quadruped robots need to navigate narrow and cluttered spaces. Existing planners use conservative approximations. This paper presents SCAN-Planner, a spatial collision-aware local planning framework using a yaw-aware twin-cylinder footprint and projected A* search in a 3D occupancy map. It enables whole-body collision evaluation with sparse queries. Experiments show successful navigation in tight spaces and overhanging structures, improving quadruped mobility.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Current local planners for quadrupeds are conservative in tight spaces and cannot reason about overhangs.
llm_ranked.method_en: SCAN-Planner uses a yaw-aware footprint and projected A* in 3D occupancy maps for whole-body collision checking.
llm_ranked.result_en: The planner enables navigation through narrow passages and under overhangs in real-world tests.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: SCAN-Planner improves spatial reasoning for quadruped navigation in complex environments.

llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: Safety dataset for human-injury prevention in embodied foundation models

llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: watermarking method for vision-language-action models to protect intellectual property
llm_ranked.tldr_en: VLA models are valuable IP when deployed as black-box services. This paper proposes a keyed latent-provenance verification method that fingerprints the policy through the noise seed used during generation. This allows ownership verification without modifying the model's behavior or performance. The method is applicable to both VLA and world-action models, providing a practical IP protection mechanism.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Deployed VLA models are valuable intellectual property that need protection against unauthorized use.
llm_ranked.method_en: Fingerprint the policy by swapping the Gaussian noise seed with a keyed one that does not affect output distribution.
llm_ranked.result_en: Enables ownership verification without altering model performance or behavior.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Watermarking is feasible for generative robot models, offering IP protection for deployed VLA systems.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: vision language action model for robot control

llm_ranked.evidence_en: depth completion for manipulation of non-Lambertian objects
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: depth completion for manipulation of non-Lambertian objects
llm_ranked.tldr_en: AISPO addresses depth reliability for robotic manipulation of transparent and specular objects. It combines multi-scale RGB-D feature fusion with an affine-invariant shape prior. The approach emphasizes physical plausibility over average accuracy to mitigate catastrophic depth failures. It aims to improve grasp success in challenging sensing conditions.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Depth measurements are often corrupted for non-Lambertian objects, causing manipulation failures.
llm_ranked.method_en: Proposes AISPO, a depth completion framework with multi-scale fusion and affine-invariant shape prior.
llm_ranked.result_en: Improves depth reliability and physical plausibility for challenging objects, reducing invalid grasp poses.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Enhances manipulation robustness in challenging sensing conditions, applicable to dexterous manipulation.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: robot manipulation with dexterous hands

llm_ranked.evidence_en: introduces R2D-RL a multi-agent reinforcement learning environment for RoboCup 2D soccer
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: introduces R2D-RL a multi-agent reinforcement learning environment for RoboCup 2D soccer
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Robot soccer is a challenging testbed for multi-agent reinforcement learning. R2D-RL bridges RCSS2D and HELIOS clients to a Python MARL interface, supporting full-field and scenario-based training with configurable opponents. It provides a reinforcement learning environment for robot control tasks in soccer.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: To provide a reinforcement learning environment for multi-agent robot control in soccer.
llm_ranked.method_en: R2D-RL connects RCSS2D and HELIOS to Python MARL via shared-memory communication and cycle-level synchronization.
llm_ranked.result_en: Supports full-field and scenario-based training with configurable opponents.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: R2D-RL enables modern MARL workflows for robot soccer.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: reinforcement learning for robot control

llm_ranked.evidence_en: foundation model for robot control safety
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: foundation model for robot control safety
llm_ranked.tldr_en: FEARL addresses safety verification of foundation models for robot control by decomposing the policy into a large perception/planning controller and a small verifiable safety module, enabling formal analysis of safety properties.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Foundation models are opaque and hard to verify for safety-critical robot tasks.
llm_ranked.method_en: Modular decomposition into a large Controller and a small Safety module with bounded context.
llm_ranked.result_en: Allows verification of safety properties while retaining rich perception.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Enables safe deployment of foundation models in robotics.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: robot foundation model for generalist robotics

llm_ranked.evidence_en: VLM for robot manipulation parameter identification
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: VLM for robot manipulation parameter identification
llm_ranked.tldr_en: RigPI uses a vision-language model to seed differentiable simulation for identifying dynamic parameters of rigid bodies during robot interaction, improving accuracy over purely data-driven approaches.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Accurate physical parameter identification is hard due to noise and limited priors.
llm_ranked.method_en: Integrates VLM semantic priors with force-torque and motion data in differentiable simulation.
llm_ranked.result_en: Improves parameter identification accuracy for manipulation.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Provides a framework for more faithful digital twins.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: Large scale robot learning techniques for robotic manipulation tasks

llm_ranked.evidence_en: GPU-accelerated MPC solver for robot control
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: GPU-accelerated MPC solver for robot control
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Model Predictive Control (MPC) is widely used in robotics but must run efficiently on GPUs to scale with modern learning pipelines. TurboMPC is a differentiable MPC solver that runs entirely on GPU, supporting state and control constraints, implicit integrators, and cross-time costs. It achieves significant speedups over existing solvers while maintaining differentiability. This enables fast and scalable optimization for robot control tasks.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: MPC solvers need to be fast and differentiable to integrate with GPU-based learning pipelines in robotics.
llm_ranked.method_en: TurboMPC combines SQP, ADMM, and co-designed JAX-CUDA implementation for efficient GPU execution with differentiability.
llm_ranked.result_en: Significant speedup over existing MPC solvers on GPU while supporting expressive optimization formulations.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: GPU-based differentiable MPC enables scalable real-time control for robotics.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: reinforcement learning for robot control

llm_ranked.evidence_en: safe local navigation for Ackermann-steered robots
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: safe local navigation for Ackermann-steered robots
llm_ranked.tldr_en: This paper proposes a control framework for safe local navigation of Ackermann-steered robots in unmapped environments. It computes safest heading using local obstacle detections and constructs bounding lines via convex optimization. A feedback-linearizing controller regulates the robot to follow these lines. The approach does not require a global goal.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: Need for safe navigation in unmapped environments without global goals.
llm_ranked.method_en: Uses convex optimization to construct bounding lines and feedback linearization control.
llm_ranked.result_en: Achieves safe local navigation with obstacle avoidance.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Provides a robust navigation method for Ackermann-steered robots.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: What are current challenges in sim to real transfer for robot navigation?

llm_ranked.evidence_en: presents a decentralized cooperative localisation algorithm for mobile robot teams
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: presents a decentralized cooperative localisation algorithm for mobile robot teams
llm_ranked.tldr_en: Localisation of robot teams is essential for navigation. This paper presents a fully decentralized algorithm that relies only on local odometry, sparse inter-agent ranging, and short-range communication. It does not require anchor infrastructure or controlling robot motion, enabling flexible deployment in unstructured environments.
llm_ranked.motivation_en: To provide an anchor-less decentralized localisation solution for robot teams in unstructured environments.
llm_ranked.method_en: A cooperative localisation algorithm using only local odometry, sparse ranging, and communication.
llm_ranked.result_en: Achieves localisation without external infrastructure or motion control constraints.
llm_ranked.conclusion_en: Enables flexible robot team localisation for navigation tasks.
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: What are current challenges in sim to real transfer for robot navigation?
